beats.speakable_text: If a craving before the reason is familiar, this craving pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun addiction usually makes the loop more expensive. More secrecy, more bargaining with yourself. And less distance from the next craving.

The craving cycle often persists because the brain has learned a fast, reliable way to change how a hard moment feels. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
Try this. Name the craving out loud, set a ten-minute delay. And do one different action in that window. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. A craving is a wave with a shape and a ceiling, not a command.
